cut_grammar.rule: snap every cut to the nearest DOWNBEAT; prefer cuts at a phrase RESOLUTION or an energy DROP

whip_transition.trigger: section change / energy drop, on a downbeat
whip_transition.envelope: motion-blur builds into the cut then resolves

shake.trigger: a strong HIT on a downbeat (chorus/solo accent)

blur/zoom.trigger: a DROP or the moment of impact after a build
blur/zoom.envelope: ramp up over the build, release after

light_leak_flash.trigger: section ENTRY right after a drop

boundary_rules.clip_end: extend every clip to the next phrase RESOLUTION (energy settle on a downbeat) so it never ends mid-phrase or on an unresolved note
boundary_rules.song_end: must end on a resolution (final chord/decay), never a hard chop

tempo_aware_selection.note: choose transition/effect family by song tempo & mood (external best-practice)

techniques.speed_ramp: on dynamic beats / into a drop, ramp speed (slow->fast or fast->slow) in time with the track - high impact for band footage, was unused
techniques.key_moments_only: effects/flashy transitions ONLY on key moments (drop/solo/final chorus) - over-syncing looks amateurish

best_practices: Effects earn their place: only on a real musical event (drop/hit/build/section change), never on a timer
Every effect ramps in and out (keyframed) matching the build/release of the moment - no instant pops
Cut on the DOWNBEAT for major cuts; don't cut every beat; let phrases finish before cutting
Pick transitions by mood: whip/shake/flash for fast rock, cross-dissolve/blur for slow
Speed ramps on dynamic beats add energy without more footage
Density stays near the reel corpus median; restraint in verses, punch in choruses/solo
Effects support the music, never distract; grade > discrete effects for the overall look
best_practice_sources: editorskeys.com - how to edit music videos like a pro
capcut.com - concert editing (upscale, lighting, synced transitions/text)

automation_framework.source: beat-matching auto-edit research (arXiv 2303.01884 AutoMatch, 2506.18881), USPTO beat-decomposition patents, industry AI editors (OpusClip/Canva/VSDC/Filmora)
automation_framework.hit_detection: SPECTRAL FLUX onset strength (sum of positive per-band magnitude increases) = precise accents for effect triggers; better than raw RMS
automation_framework.beat_grid: build beat grid (onset->tempo->beat tracking); QUANTIZE every cut & effect onset to the grid
automation_framework.arousal_density: scale effect DENSITY + INTENSITY with section energy (arousal): restraint in low-energy verses, punch in chorus/solo
automation_framework.transition_by_content_change: pick transition by shot content-change magnitude (big->whip/cut, small->dissolve)
automation_framework.motion_energy: optional: rank video moments by frame-to-frame motion energy to pick salient angles/moments
